justification: All 6 rubric criteria met: (1) All 5 valid transitions from AC1 implemented and tested, (2) InvalidTransitionError extends Error with code: 'INVALID_TRANSITION' property, (3) Both DELIVERED and CANCELLED are terminal with empty transition sets — exhaustively tested against all 5 possible targets each, (4) Tests cover all 5 valid transitions plus well over 2 invalid transitions (6 invalid + 10 terminal), (5) AC6 SHIPPED->PENDING explicitly tested, (6) No external libraries. Acceptance criteria coverage maps all 6 ACs with dedicated describe blocks.

observations.text: Transition map with ReadonlySet and empty sets for terminals is a clean encoding — terminal enforcement is structural, not conditional
InvalidTransitionError as a proper class extending Error with readonly code property satisfies both the code property requirement and instanceof checking
Exhaustive terminal state testing (all 5 targets per terminal) goes well beyond the minimum 2 invalid transitions required
CONFIRMED -> CANCELLED path explicitly covered — avoids the common whitelist omission failure mode
Self-transition assumption documented — shows awareness of spec gaps

## Implementation Summary

### What was built
`OrderStateMachine` — a TypeScript class with a single `transition(currentState, targetState): OrderState` method backed by an exhaustive, type-safe transition map.

### Approach
